memes.label: "With the Empress Dowager of China"
memes.description: "By Katharine A. Carl."

memes.label: Uncopyright
memes.description: "> May you do good and not evil."

memes.label: "American Legation"
memes.description: "The United States diplomatic mission in Peking, where Katherine Carl resided during the portrait commission, 1903–1904."

memes.label: "Ancestor Worship"
memes.description: "The Confucian practice of venerating and making offerings to deceased ancestors, a foundational element of Chinese ritual life and court ceremony during the Qing dynasty (023-008)."

memes.label: "Ancestral Tablets"
memes.description: "Ancestral tablets — wooden plaques inscribed with the names of deceased ancestors — were the focus of Imperial ancestor worship at the Qing Court. Katherine Carl witnessed the Emperor and Empress kneeling together before these tablets at the conclusion of the Birthday ceremonies in the Sacred Hall."

memes.label: "Audience Hall"
memes.description: "The principal audience hall within the Imperial Palace where the Empress Dowager and Emperor received foreign diplomats, officials, and distinguished visitors in formal ceremony."

memes.label: "Baron Czikan"
memes.description: "Austrian Minister to China and Doyen of the Corps Diplomatique in Peking, who formally presented the ladies of the Legations at the Chinese New Year audience."

memes.label: "Belvedere"
memes.description: "A pavilion on Coal Hill marking the spot where the last Ming emperor committed suicide in 1644"

memes.label: "Book of Rites"
memes.description: "One of the Five Classics of Confucianism, the Book of Rites (Liji) codified the ceremonial protocols, filial duties, and ritual calendar that structured Qing court life. (009-011)"

memes.label: "The Boxer Rebellion's Aftermath at the Qing Court"
memes.description: "How the Qing Imperial Court navigated the aftermath of the Boxer Rebellion (1900) and the foreign occupation of Peking, as witnessed by Katherine Carl during her portrait-painting residency in 1903–1904."

relations.label: Temple of the Ten Thousand Buddhas

memes.label: "Boxer Rebellion"
memes.description: "Anti-foreign uprising of 1900 that resulted in the siege of the Peking Legation Quarter and shaped the diplomatic context of Katherine Carl's portrait commission."

memes.label: "British Legation"
memes.description: "The British diplomatic compound in Peking's Legation Quarter, which served as the primary refuge for foreigners during the Boxer Rebellion of 1900."

memes.label: "Brocade"
memes.description: "The rich figured fabric woven with raised patterns, used in Imperial Qing costume as described in Carl's detailed observations of court dress."

memes.label: "Buddhism"
memes.description: "Buddhism (佛教) — one of the three great religions of China, practiced alongside Taoism and the worship of Nature, with temples and ceremonies within the Imperial Palace precincts under the nominal headship of the Emperor. (013-004)(029-002)"

relations.label: Temple of the Ten Thousand Buddhas

memes.label: "Buddhist Worship"
memes.description: "The Buddhist religious observances at the Qing court, centred on palace temples, incense offerings, and ceremonial rites witnessed by Katherine Carl during her residence at the Sea Palace."

memes.label: "First Catholic Cathedral of Peking"
memes.description: "The first Catholic Cathedral built in Peking, constructed on land granted by the Emperor and later incorporated within the walls of the Sea Palace."

memes.label: "Chang Chih-tung"
memes.description: "A prominent Qing dynasty Viceroy and scholar-official, author of a famous ode to the Emperor Kwang-Hsu praising his filial piety, cited in Carl's endnotes."

memes.label: "Chinese Architecture"
memes.description: "Traditional Chinese architectural principles and forms as observed and described by Katherine Carl in her memoir, with particular attention to the tent-origin theory of Chinese building design and the temples, palaces, and walls of Peking."

relations.label: Temple of the Ten Thousand Buddhas

memes.label: "Chinese City"
memes.description: "The southern outer district of Peking, containing the great Nature-worship temples including the Temple of Heaven."

memes.label: "Chinese Court"
memes.description: "The Imperial Court of the Qing dynasty under the Empress Dowager Cixi, centered on the Forbidden City and its associated palaces, as experienced by Katherine Carl during her 1903–1904 residence."

memes.label: "Chinese Cuisine"
memes.description: "The culinary tradition of the Qing court, characterised by elaborate multi-course meals served in tall porcelain dishes, observed by Katherine Carl at the Empress Dowager's table with a mixture of curiosity and admiration."

memes.label: "Chinese New Year at the Qing Court"
memes.description: "The Chinese New Year celebrations at the Qing Court in 1904, including formal diplomatic audiences, lantern festivals, fireworks, and the move to the Sea Palace, as witnessed by Katherine Carl."
aliases: Lunar New Year at the Palace

memes.label: "Chinese Official"
memes.description: "The mandarin scholar-official class who staffed the Qing imperial bureaucracy, distinguished by a rigid nine-rank hierarchy, distinctive costume, and a culture of competitive examination."

memes.label: "Chinese Theater"
memes.description: "The tradition of theatrical performance at the Qing court, centred on the great Palace Theater where elaborate productions with music, costume, and acrobatics marked festivals and imperial celebrations."

memes.label: "Chinese Tradition"
memes.description: "The body of traditional Chinese customs, rituals, and cultural practices observed at the late Qing Court and described in Katherine Carl's memoir, encompassing ceremonies, dress, etiquette, and seasonal observances."

memes.label: "Chopsticks"
memes.description: "The traditional Chinese eating utensil, a pair of tapered sticks used for picking up food, observed by Katherine Carl at the Empress Dowager's court as an object of both daily use and elegant craftsmanship."

memes.label: "Cixi's Portrait: From Commission to St. Louis"
memes.description: "The story of the first Western portrait ever painted of the Empress Dowager Cixi — from its diplomatic conception in 1903 through Katherine Carl's nine-month residency to the painting's journey to the 1904 St. Louis World's Fair."

memes.label: "Coal Hill"
memes.description: "An artificial hill in Peking overlooking the Imperial City, surmounted by the Dagoba and marking the site of the last Ming emperor's suicide."

memes.label: "Confucianism"
memes.description: "Confucianism is a system of ethics and a philosophy which governs Chinese life and court ritual, followed by the Empress Dowager and the Qing court. (029-011)"

memes.label: "Confucius"
memes.description: "Confucius (c. 551–479 BCE), the great Chinese Sage and ethical teacher whose philosophy directed the lives and laws of the Chinese people for nearly twenty-five hundred years and governed all rites at the Qing court. (013-005)"

relations.label: Temple of the Ten Thousand Buddhas

memes.label: "Edwin H. Conger"
memes.description: "Edwin H. Conger, United States Minister to China (1898-1905), whose wife Sarah Pike Conger facilitated Katherine Carl's commission to paint the Empress Dowager's portrait."

memes.label: "Corps Diplomatique"
memes.description: "The community of foreign diplomatic representatives in Peking during the late Qing period, serving as the primary channel between foreign powers and the Chinese Court."

memes.label: "Court Ceremony"
memes.description: "The formal ceremonies and rituals of the Qing Imperial Court, including the morning salutation, kotow, and Emperor's Birthday celebrations observed by Katherine Carl."

memes.label: "Court Costume"
memes.description: "The formal Court costume of the Qing dynasty was an elaborate system of silk gowns embroidered with the Double Dragon, regulated by rank, marital status, and season. Katherine Carl described it in detail, noting the rigid enforcement of every detail by the Empress Dowager herself."

memes.label: "Court Etiquette"
memes.description: "The elaborate system of protocol, deportment, and ritual propriety governing behavior at the Qing Imperial Court, as observed and documented by Katherine Carl during her residence at the Summer Palace in 1903–1904."

memes.label: "Dagoba"
memes.description: "The distinctive white Buddhist monument atop Coal Hill, visible in all views of the Imperial City of Peking"

memes.label: "Double Dragon"
memes.description: "The paramount imperial emblem of the Qing dynasty, a rampant pair of five-clawed dragons representing eternal imperial authority in costume, furniture, and architecture."

memes.label: "Dr. Matignon"
memes.description: "A French doctor in Peking quoted in Carl's endnotes regarding the causes of the Boxer Rebellion, arguing that European misunderstanding of China was a primary factor."

memes.label: "Dragon Wall"
memes.description: "A famous landmark structure in Peking, noted by Katherine Carl during her travels through the city to the Palace."

memes.label: "Eastern Palace"
memes.description: "One of the two main palace complexes within the Imperial precincts, historically associated with the Eastern Empress Dowager (Ci'an) and later used for ceremonial functions during Katherine Carl's residence."

memes.label: "Embroidery"
memes.description: "The decorative needlework art used extensively in Qing Court costume, from gold embroidery on Court Ladies' dresses to silk thread designs on shoes and robes."

memes.label: "Emperor's Birthday"
memes.description: "The ceremonial celebration of Emperor Kwang-Hsu's 32nd birthday at the Summer Palace in August 1903, witnessed firsthand by Katherine Carl—the first foreigner ever invited to the event."

memes.label: "Emperor Guangxu"
memes.description: "Alternate romanization (pinyin) for the Guangxu Emperor (1871–1908), the titular Emperor of China during Katherine Carl's residence at the Court. See [[emperor-kwang-hsu|Emperor Kwang-Hsu]] for the main entry."

memes.label: "Emperor Hsien-Feng"
memes.description: "Emperor Hsien-Feng (Xianfeng, 1831–1861), the seventh Qing emperor, husband of the Empress Dowager Cixi and father of the Tung-Chih Emperor, whose death at Jehol precipitated the power struggles that defined late Qing politics."

memes.label: "Emperor Kwang-Hsu"
memes.description: "The Guangxu Emperor (光绪帝, 1871–1908), the titular Emperor of China during Katherine Carl's residence at the Court, reigning under the co-regency of the Empress Dowager Cixi (008-003)."

memes.label: "Emperor Tung-Chih"
memes.description: "Emperor Tung-Chih (Tongzhi, 1856–1875), the only son of the Empress Dowager Cixi and the Hsien-Feng Emperor, whose childless death at nineteen triggered the succession crisis that brought his cousin Kwang-Hsu to the throne."

memes.label: "The Young Empress"
memes.description: "The young Empress (Longyu), first wife of the Emperor Kwang-Hsu, daughter of Duke Chow and niece of the Empress Dowager Cixi, who served as the first Lady of the Court after Her Majesty and appears constantly at the Empress Dowager's side throughout the narrative. (005-002)"

memes.label: "Empress Dowager Cixi"
memes.description: "Empress Dowager Cixi (慈禧太后, 1835–1908), the de facto ruler of China during the late Qing dynasty, as portrayed in Katherine Carl's first-hand account of painting her portrait [P04-001]."

memes.label: "Empress Dowager's Birthday"
memes.description: "The 69th birthday celebration of Empress Dowager Cixi in November 1903 at the Sea Palace, featuring elaborate rites, theatrical performances, and the formal prostrations of the Emperor and the entire Qing Court."

memes.label: "Empress of the Eastern Palace"
memes.description: "Co-Regent Empress of China during the Tongzhi and early Guangxu reigns, known as the \"Literary Empress\" for her scholarly pursuits. Died in 1881. (015-002)"

memes.label: "European Circus at the Palace"
memes.description: "A European circus performance held in the grounds of the Summer Palace in 1903, providing Katherine Carl with a rare opportunity to observe the Manchu princes and officials together."

memes.label: "Festival of the Harvest Moon"
memes.description: "The Mid-Autumn Festival celebrations at the Qing Court in 1903, featuring theatrical performances of the Moon legend, illuminated ceremonies at the Temple of the Ten Thousand Buddhas, and a lantern-lit procession across the lake, as witnessed by Katherine Carl."

memes.label: "Filial Piety"
memes.description: "The Confucian virtue of filial devotion and reverence toward one's parents, identified in Katherine Carl's memoir as one of the Emperor Kwang-Hsu's greatest qualities."

memes.label: "First Audience with the Empress Dowager"
memes.description: "Katherine Carl's first private audience with the Empress Dowager Cixi on August 5, 1903, at the Summer Palace -- the unprecedented event that began her nine-month residency at the Chinese Imperial Court."

memes.label: "Flaming Pearl"
memes.description: "The Flaming Pearl — emblematic of the 'Unattainable' — was the central symbol of the Qing Dynasty, eternally pursued by the Double Dragon in Imperial iconography. It appeared on thrones, pennants, Court gowns, and throughout the Palace, representing perfection that remains forever beyond reach."

memes.label: "Flowery Mountain"
memes.description: "A terraced hillside in the grounds of the Summer Palace, renowned for its spectacular peony displays during the flowering season."

memes.label: "Garden Party at the Summer Palace"
memes.description: "A formal garden party hosted by the Empress Dowager at the Summer Palace for the ladies and gentlemen of the foreign Legations in Peking, featuring diplomatic audiences, a lake tour, and refreshments at the Marble Boat."

memes.label: "Glacis"
memes.description: "An open area in the Legation Quarter of Peking, situated between the British Legation and the Marble Bridge, traversed by Katherine Carl on her daily journey to the Palace."

memes.label: "Grain-bearing Canal"
memes.description: "A canal in Peking crossed by a stone bridge en route to the Winter Palace"

memes.label: "Grand Council"
memes.description: "The supreme policy-making body of the Qing Empire, presided over by Prince Ching during Katherine Carl's residence at the Court, 1903–1904."

memes.label: "Grand Secretary"
memes.description: "The Grand Secretary (内阁大学士) — a senior official position in the Qing imperial government, one of the highest-ranking ministers who attended daily Audiences with Their Majesties and held a privileged, permanently reserved kneeling cushion nearest the Throne dais. (016-006)(016-008)"

memes.label: "Great Audience Hall"
memes.description: "The principal ceremonial hall in the Summer Palace where the Emperor and Empress Dowager held official audiences, 1903–1904."

memes.label: "Great Hall of Ceremonies"
memes.description: "The principal ceremonial hall in the imperial palace where Qing court officials presented formal homage and congratulations to the Emperor."

memes.label: "Hall of the Mongolian Princes"
memes.description: "A magnificent ceremonial hall in the Sea Palace used for receiving the Mongolian Princes during their annual tribute visit to the Qing court."

memes.label: "Hsien-Liang-Hsu"
memes.description: "The Temple of the Loyal and Virtuous in Peking, formerly the residence of Li-Hung-Chang and later the home of the Yu-Keng family after their return from Paris."
aliases: Temple of the Loyal and Virtuous

memes.label: "Hundred Days' Reform"
memes.description: "The sweeping reform movement initiated by Emperor Kwang-Hsu in 1898, abruptly terminated by the coup d'état led by conservative forces and the Empress Dowager Cixi. (030-012)"

memes.label: "Imperial Audience Ceremony"
memes.description: "The formal ceremony by which the Empress Dowager and the Emperor received foreign diplomats, officials, and distinguished visitors in audience at the Imperial Palace."

memes.label: "Imperial Barge"
memes.description: "The Empress Dowager's personal watercraft on the lake at the Summer Palace, a traditional yellow-canopied barge towed by rowers that served as the centerpiece of the Imperial fleet for daily excursions, ceremonies, and festival night processions. (002-013)"

relations.label: garden party at the Summer Palace

memes.label: "Imperial City"
memes.description: "The innermost walled city of Peking, enclosing the Winter Palace and imperial compounds, 1903–1904."

memes.label: "Imperial Gourd"
memes.description: "A specially cultivated gourd at the Chinese Imperial Palace, emblematic of Fruitfulness and Prosperity, elevated to high perfection under the Empress Dowager Cixi's patronage. (011-012)"

memes.label: "Imperial Hymn"
memes.description: "The 'Imperial Hymn' — a solemn minor-key air played on cymbals and flutes — was the Qing Court's ceremonial music, performed whenever the Emperor and Empress Dowager processed to official functions. Katherine Carl described it as 'the only approach to a National air' she ever heard in China."

memes.label: "Imperial Palaces of the Qing Court"
memes.description: "A comparative overview of the three principal Imperial palaces of the Qing Dynasty — the Summer Palace, Sea Palace, and Winter Palace — as experienced and described by Katherine Carl in 1903–1904."

relations.label: Temple of the Ten Thousand Buddhas

memes.label: "Imperial Plowing Ceremony"
memes.description: "An annual ceremony in which the Emperor plowed the first furrow and sowed the first seeds of the year, symbolizing the Imperial government's connection to agriculture and the land."

memes.label: "Imperial Processions at the Qing Court"
memes.description: "The ceremonial processions of the Qing Court witnessed by Katherine Carl, from the daily promenades through Palace gardens to the grand torch-lit and lantern-lit festivals, featuring hundreds of eunuchs bearing lanterns and elaborate Imperial pageantry."

memes.label: "Imperial Throne"
memes.description: "The Imperial Throne of the Qing dynasty, its physical form and ceremonial significance, as described in Katherine Carl's account of the Chinese Court."

memes.label: "Imperial Yellow"
memes.description: "The Imperial yellow color reserved exclusively for the Emperor and Empress Dowager of China, as observed in court dress and ceremonial objects."

memes.label: "Incense-Burner"
memes.description: "Bronze and golden incense-burners were central to Qing Court ritual — swung by acolytes before Imperial processions, placed on great tripods for moon ceremonies, and used in Buddhist worship at the Palace temples. Katherine Carl witnessed them in ceremonies from daily rites to the Festival of the Harvest Moon."

memes.label: "Incense-Burning"
memes.description: "The ritual burning of incense in golden censers at the Qing court, an essential element of Buddhist ceremonies, imperial processions, and ancestor veneration witnessed by Katherine Carl."

memes.label: "Invisible Deity"
memes.description: "The Invisible Deity was the highest object of worship in the purest form of Chinese religion — the worship of Nature. The Emperor, as 'Son of Heaven,' was its sole High Priest, offering semiannual sacrifices on the great white marble altar at the Temple of Heaven in Peking."

memes.label: "Jade Ornaments"
memes.description: "The use of jade in Imperial Court ornaments, accessories, and ritual objects as observed during Katherine Carl's residence at the Summer Palace."

memes.label: "Katharine A. Carl"
memes.description: "Katharine A. Carl (1865-1938), American portrait painter who resided at the Chinese Imperial Court for nine months in 1903-1904 to paint the Empress Dowager Cixi's portrait — the first Western artist ever granted such access — and author of 'With the Empress Dowager of China' (1905)."

memes.label: "Kite-Flying"
memes.description: "Kite-flying was a cherished spring pastime at the Qing Court, enjoyed by the Empress Dowager and Ladies of the Palace. Katherine Carl witnessed the first kites of the season being flown in the gardens of the Sea Palace, noting that 'High Officials and dignified literati indulge in the pastime as well as children.'"

memes.label: "Kowtow"
memes.description: "The ceremonial prostration (磕头, kētóu) — three kneelings and nine knockings of the head — performed before the Chinese Imperial throne as the ultimate gesture of submission and respect, and the focus of the most significant diplomatic conflict between China and Western powers."

memes.label: "Kublai Khan"
memes.description: "Mongol Emperor and founder of the Yuan Dynasty in China, referenced in connection with Imperial poetry and the grounds of his palace. (009-014)"

memes.label: "Lady of the Court"
memes.description: "The Manchu princesses, noblewomen, and attendants who lived at the Qing Imperial Court, as observed by Katherine Carl during her residence at the Summer Palace in 1903–1904."

memes.label: "Lady Yu"
memes.description: "Lady Yu Keng, the daughter of a former Chinese Minister abroad, who served as an interpreter and companion to the Empress Dowager, bridging the linguistic gap between the Court and its foreign visitors."

memes.label: "Lady Yu-Keng"
memes.description: "Lady Yu-Keng, wife of a former Chinese minister to Japan, served as interpreter and lady-in-waiting at the Qing Court during Katherine Carl's nine-month residence painting the Empress Dowager's portrait."

memes.label: "Lao-tze"
memes.description: "Ancient Chinese philosopher, founder of Taoism, whose doctrines — along with those of Buddha — are described in the narrative as having become encrusted with error in China. (029-011)"

memes.label: "Legation Quarter"
memes.description: "The fortified diplomatic enclave in Peking established after the Boxer Rebellion of 1900, housing the foreign legations and serving as the base for the diplomatic community during Katherine Carl's residence."

memes.label: "Legation Street"
memes.description: "A street in the Legation Quarter of Peking where new diplomatic compounds were constructed after the Boxer Rebellion of 1900."

memes.label: "Li Hung-chang"
memes.description: "The preeminent Chinese statesman and diplomat of the late Qing period, who negotiated the peace after the Boxer Rebellion and whose former residence served as Katherine Carl's first lodging in Peking."

memes.label: "Li Lien-ying"
memes.description: "Li Lien-ying, the Empress Dowager Cixi's Chief Eunuch, a figure of exceptional ability and considerable power at the Qing Court as described in Katherine Carl's memoir."

memes.label: "Manchu Banner"
memes.description: "The foundational military-social organization of the Manchu Qing Dynasty, composed of eight ranked banners including the Yellow Banner and White Banner. (005-002)(030-002)"

memes.label: "Manchu Coiffure"
memes.description: "The elaborate hairstyle worn by Manchu women of the Qing court, meticulously described by Katherine Carl in her memoir of 1903–1904."

memes.label: "Manchu Customs"
memes.description: "The distinctive customs, dress, and cultural practices of the Manchu people as observed at the Chinese Imperial Court during the late Qing dynasty."

memes.label: "Manchu Dynasty"
memes.description: "The Manchu Dynasty, officially called the Dynasty of the Great Purity, was the ruling imperial dynasty of China during the period covered by the book, with the Empress Dowager Cixi as its de facto ruler. (008-003)"

memes.label: "Manchu Headdress"
memes.description: "The elaborate formal headdress worn by Manchu women at the Imperial Court, featuring a winged bat-shaped frame adorned with jewels, pearls, flowers, and tassels, as described by Katherine Carl."

memes.label: "Manchu Shoes"
memes.description: "Distinctive Manchu women's shoes with six-inch-high platform soles, worn at the Imperial Court by the Empress Dowager and Manchu court ladies, giving them a characteristic gait and stature."

memes.label: "Manchuria"
memes.description: "The ancestral homeland of the Manchu people and cradle of the Qing Dynasty, which became a focal point of Russian and Japanese imperial rivalry during the Empress Dowager Cixi's reign. (023-007)"

memes.label: "Marble Boat"
memes.description: "The Marble Boat (石舫, *Shí Fǎng*) was a famous lakeside pavilion at the Summer Palace, built of white marble in the form of a Western-style pleasure boat — a whimsical architectural folly that was one of the most photographed landmarks of the Imperial grounds."

memes.label: "Marble Bridge"
memes.description: "A graceful white marble bridge spanning the northern lake in the Sea Palace, admired by Katherine Carl as a architectural landmark, 1903–1904."

memes.label: "Moon Festival"
memes.description: "The traditional Chinese Mid-Autumn Festival as celebrated at the Imperial Court, featuring moon-viewing, offerings, and the distribution of moon cakes, described in Katherine Carl's memoir."

memes.label: "Mrs. Conger"
memes.description: "Wife of the American Minister to China and key facilitator of Katherine Carl's portrait commission by the Empress Dowager Cixi, 1903–1904."

memes.label: "Nail-Protectors"
memes.description: "Ornamental shields worn over the long fingernails of Qing imperial women, crafted from gold, jade, or tortoiseshell, signalling high status by demonstrating that the wearer performed no manual labour."

memes.label: "Official Beads"
memes.description: "Official beads (朝珠, *cháozhū*) were the ceremonial necklaces worn by Princes, Officials, and their wives in Qing Court dress. Katherine Carl noted that 'the Emperor and Empress Dowager are the only members of the Court who wear neither the Double Dragon on their Court dress, nor the official beads' — marking their unique status."

memes.label: "Palace Eunuch"
memes.description: "The institution of eunuchs at the Qing court — castrated male servants who formed an army of attendants, managed the imperial household, and were a constant presence in Katherine Carl's account of palace life."

memes.label: "Palace of the Emperor's Father"
memes.description: "The palace of Prince Ch'un (the Seventh Prince), used as Katherine Carl's residence during her portrait commission, 1903–1904."

memes.label: "Pearl Ornaments"
memes.description: "The use of pearls in Imperial Qing costume and jewelry, from embroidered gowns to Court Ladies' headdresses, as observed by Katherine Carl."

memes.label: "Peking"
memes.description: "Capital of the Qing Empire and seat of the foreign Legation Quarter, 1903–1904."

memes.label: "Pekingese"
memes.description: "The Pekingese pug was a treasured breed of dog kept by the Empress Dowager and the Qing court, bred with great care for their symmetrical markings, silky coats, and intelligence. (006-006)"

memes.label: "Portrait for St. Louis"
memes.description: "The large official portrait of the Empress Dowager Cixi painted by Katharine Carl for the 1904 St. Louis Exposition (Louisiana Purchase Exposition), which became the centerpiece of the Chinese exhibit and a significant diplomatic gesture between China and the United States. (P04-001)"

memes.label: "Present-Giving"
memes.description: "The elaborate system of ceremonial gift exchange at the Qing court, through which officials, courtiers, and foreign visitors expressed loyalty, gratitude, and social obligation to the throne."

memes.label: "Prince Adalbert of Prussia"
memes.description: "A young Prince of Prussia who was received in special audience by the Empress Dowager and Emperor during his visit to Peking, accompanied by the German Minister."

memes.label: "Prince Ching"
memes.description: "Prince of the First Rank and Prime Minister of the Qing Empire, head of the Grand Council and the Wai-Wu-Pu during Katherine Carl's residence at the Chinese Court, 1903–1904."

memes.label: "Prince Ch'un (the Seventh Prince)"
memes.description: "Prince Ch'un (1840–1891), the Seventh Prince, was the father of Emperor Kwang-Hsu, brother of Emperor Hsien-Feng, and a trusted adviser to the Empress Dowager Cixi during the co-regency. His palace at the Summer Palace housed Katherine Carl during her portrait residency."

memes.label: "Prince Kung"
memes.description: "Prince Kung (Prince Gong), a late Qing prince and statesman whose name became synonymous with progress and reform in Carl's account of the Chinese Court."

memes.label: "Prince Pu Lun"
memes.description: "Imperial Commissioner and personal representative of the Empress Dowager and Emperor at the 1904 St. Louis Exposition, who received the sacred portrait upon its arrival."

memes.label: "Prince Su"
memes.description: "A Manchu prince of the Imperial clan who accompanied the Court on its flight to Singan Fu during the Boxer Rebellion and later recorded the hardships of that journey."

memes.label: "Prince Tuan"
memes.description: "Prince Tuan (Duan), a Manchu prince and father of the heir-designate to the Qing throne, whose support gave the Boxer movement legitimacy and political force at the Court."

memes.label: "Princess Imperial"
memes.description: "The Empress Dowager Cixi's adopted daughter and First Lady of the Court, the highest-ranking princess in Katherine Carl's account of life at the Chinese Court."

memes.label: "Princess Schun"
memes.description: "A clever Manchu princess, widow of Grand Secretary Yung Lu and sister of the young Empress, known at the Imperial Court for her intelligence and wit."

memes.label: "Propriety"
memes.description: "The Confucian concept of ritual propriety (li) governing behavior at the Qing Court, including correct forms of address, gesture, and ceremony."

memes.label: "Ruyie (Jade Emblem)"
memes.description: "The *ruyie* (如意, *rúyì*) — a jade emblem of Good Luck — was presented to the Emperor on ceremonial occasions by Princes and Nobles. Katherine Carl observed it at the Emperor's Birthday and corrected the Western misconception that it was a scepter of Imperial authority."

memes.label: "The Sacred Picture"
memes.description: "The term used at the Qing court for Katherine Carl's portrait of the Empress Dowager Cixi, treated as a quasi-sacred object and handled with elaborate ceremonial protocol."

memes.label: "Sea Palace"
memes.description: "Imperial palace complex in central Peking, used by the Empress Dowager Cixi as an alternative residence, 1903–1904."

memes.label: "Semiannual Sacrifices at the Qing Court"
memes.description: "The semiannual ritual sacrifices performed at the Qing Court, including the Confucian memorial ceremony at the Summer Palace temple and the Emperor's solstice sacrifices at the Temple of Heaven, as witnessed by Katherine Carl."

memes.label: "Silk Court Dress"
memes.description: "The elaborate silk costumes worn at the Chinese Imperial Court, including the stiff transparent summer silk and Imperial yellow brocade described by Katherine Carl."

memes.label: "Singan Fu"
memes.description: "The ancient capital of China, known today as Xi'an, where the Imperial Court fled during the Boxer Rebellion of 1900 and endured severe hardships, as recorded in Katherine Carl's memoir."

memes.label: "Sir Chentung Liang Cheng"
memes.description: "Chinese Minister to the United States who formally presented the Empress Dowager's portrait to President Theodore Roosevelt in Washington, D.C."

memes.label: "Sir Claude MacDonald"
memes.description: "British Minister to China who reported favorably on the Empress Dowager's first audience to foreigners, describing her as a kind and courteous hostess."

memes.label: "Sir Robert Hart"
memes.description: "Sir Robert Hart (1835–1911), Inspector-General of the Imperial Chinese Maritime Customs Service, a towering figure in late Qing administration and the dedicatee of Katherine Carl's memoir."

memes.label: "Son of Heaven"
memes.description: "Son of Heaven (天子) — the sacred title of the Emperor of China, who as the sole High Priest of Nature worship alone performed the great semiannual sacrifices to the Invisible Deity and acted as expiator for the Empire's afflictions. (001-007)(029-004)"

memes.label: "Spirit-Stairway"
memes.description: "The 'spirit-stairway' was a distinctive architectural feature of Chinese temples and palaces — a central marble ramp carved with the Double Dragon, never touched by mortal feet, that ran up the middle of ceremonial staircases like a 'beautiful heavy carpet.'"

memes.label: "Spring Awakening Ceremony"
memes.description: "A traditional ceremony at the Imperial Palace celebrating the first signs of spring, featuring the presentation of the season's first radishes and lettuce shoots to the Empress Dowager."

memes.label: "St. Louis Exposition"
memes.description: "The 1904 Louisiana Purchase Exposition in St. Louis, for which Katherine Carl was commissioned to paint the Empress Dowager Cixi's portrait."

memes.label: "Steam-Launch"
memes.description: "Steam-powered launches used on the lake at the Summer Palace, introduced alongside the traditional Imperial barge as the Empress Dowager embraced modern modes of locomotion. (013-002)"

memes.label: "Summer Palace"
memes.description: "The Summer Palace (颐和园), the Empress Dowager Cixi's favorite residence sixteen miles from Peking, where Katherine Carl painted her portraits across nine months in 1903–1904."

relations.label: Temple of the Ten Thousand Buddhas

memes.label: "Taoism"
memes.description: "Taoism (道教) — one of the three great religions of China, an ancient philosophical and religious tradition whose ceremonies were celebrated in the Palace temples under the nominal headship of the Emperor, alongside Buddhism and the worship of Nature. (013-004)(029-002)"

memes.label: "Tartar City"
memes.description: "The Manchu district of Peking forming the inner section of the city, containing the Legation Quarter and bordering the Imperial City walls."

memes.label: "Temple of Agriculture"
memes.description: "One of the great Nature-worship temples in Peking where the Emperor, as Son of Heaven, performed agricultural rites for the prosperity of the empire."

memes.label: "Temple of Heaven"
memes.description: "The most imposing of the temples in the Park of the Temple of Heaven complex in the Chinese City of Peking"

relations.label: Temple of the Ten Thousand Buddhas

memes.label: "Temple of the Ten Thousand Buddhas"
memes.description: "The hilltop temple in the Summer Palace crowned with glazed yellow-tile Buddhas, site of semiannual Confucian sacrifices, 1903–1904."
aliases: Great Temple of the Ten Thousand Buddhas

memes.label: "Theodore Roosevelt"
memes.description: "Twenty-sixth President of the United States, who received the presentation of the Empress Dowager's portrait on behalf of the United States Government in Washington. (034-015)"

memes.label: "Tientsin"
memes.description: "Tientsin (Tianjin, 天津) was a major treaty port and the gateway to Peking during the late Qing dynasty. It figures in Katherine Carl's memoir as the source of the European circus brought to the Summer Palace and the seat of the Viceroy Yuan Shih-Kai."

memes.label: "Wai-Wu-Pu"
memes.description: "The Wai-Wu-Pu (Waiwubu), the Chinese Foreign Office during the late Qing dynasty, responsible for managing foreign relations and the interface between the Imperial Court and the diplomatic corps in Peking."

memes.label: "Water Gate"
memes.description: "A gate in the wall between the Tartar City and the Legation Quarter of Peking, opened by the Allied forces in 1900 during the Boxer Rebellion."

memes.label: "Western Hills"
memes.description: "The Western Hills (西山, *Xīshān*) were the mountain range visible from Peking and the Summer Palace, forming a picturesque backdrop to the Imperial gardens. In Katherine Carl's account, they framed the landscape she viewed daily from the Palace grounds."

memes.label: "Western Palace"
memes.description: "One of the two main palace complexes within the Imperial precincts of Peking, historically the residence of the Empress Dowager Cixi and the center of her power during the late Qing dynasty."
aliases: Palace of the Western Empress Dowager

memes.label: "Winter Palace"
memes.description: "The winter residence of the Qing imperial court within the Imperial City of Peking, described by Katharine Carl during her stay painting the Empress Dowager's portrait in 1903–1904."

memes.label: "Yung Lu"
memes.description: "Yung Lu (Jung Lu), Grand Secretary of the Qing Court, a powerful statesman whose widow and daughter remained prominent figures at the Court during Katherine Carl's residence."
